evt-001.summary: Spoofed call from +916511361582 impersonated a CBI officer threatening arrest. Victim was asked to transfer ₹2 lakh and not tell family. Call entity connects to 6 other nodes in the fraud graph including 2 flagged mule accounts. 3 spoofed calls detected within the 2-hop subgraph.

evidenceItems.snippet: "Yeh CBI headquarters se bol rahe hain. Aapke naam arrest warrant hai. Abhi ₹2 lakh transfer karo warna giraftari hogi. Kisi ko mat batana..."
Caller ID spoofed to appear as Delhi Police landline. True origin traced to VOIP node connected to 5 other scam numbers in synthetic dataset.
2 transactions linked to this number: ₹49,500 and ₹48,200 — both below ₹50,000 threshold. Flagged as structuring (smurfing) activity.

evt-001.recommendedAction: Freeze accounts ACCT-DL-8821 and ACCT-DL-3392. File SAR with FIU-IND. Alert victim and report to cybercrime.gov.in helpline 1930.

evt-002.summary: Counterfeit ₹500 note detected at Dadar Market merchant. Matches known print signature — missing watermark, incorrect security thread fluorescence. This is the 4th report within 2 km in 48 hours, indicating coordinated distribution.

evidenceItems.snippet: Mahatma Gandhi watermark absent when held against light. Security thread does not carry 'भारत' text. Latent image of denomination numeral not visible at 45°.
4th counterfeit report within 2 km radius in past 48 hours. Hotspot intensity 0.78 — HIGH severity zone in Mumbai geospatial cluster.

evt-002.recommendedAction: Dispatch local unit to Dadar Market. Collect physical note for RBI forensic examination. Alert nearby merchants via SafeNet bulletin.

evt-006.summary: Three merchants in Hazratganj bazaar reported high-quality counterfeit ₹2000 notes within 90 minutes. Notes lack colour-shifting numeral (should shift magenta→green) and latent image. Pattern matches a known printer ring operating in UP.

evidenceItems.snippet: ₹2000 note: colour-shifting numeral panel shows no shift. Ashoka Pillar emblem on reverse shows reduced tactile relief. Paper weight below 100g/m² standard.
3 reports from MERCH-UP-112, MERCH-UP-443, MERCH-UP-992 — all within 500m, 90-minute window. Classic coordinated distribution pass.
MERCH-UP-443 previously flagged in 2025 for receiving counterfeit ₹500 notes. Repeat-victim or potential insider.

evt-006.recommendedAction: Immediate field unit dispatch to Hazratganj. Seize all ₹2000 notes at three locations. Escalate to State Currency Crime Cell, Lucknow.
